events.title: Button Book Club
events.description: Do you love to read but hate deadlines? Or are you just too busy to attend traditional book club meetings? If so, our Button Book Club is perfect for you! Each month there will be a new book to read and a brand new pin to collect! How It Works Pick Your Book! Each month will feature a new Easy/Picture, Beginning Reader, Middle Grade, and Teen book. Simply pick which level you want to read and check out the book! Share Your Thoughts! Come back anytime during the month and write/draw your favorite part of the book. Get Your Pin! Each book will have it's own special, collectible pin. Participate every month to collect them all! Plus, get a free lanyard with your first pin to show them off! June Books: Easy/Picture: Fairy Walk by Gaia Cornwall Beginning Readers: Danger in the Dragon's Den by Megan Reyes Middle Grade: Sona and the Golden Beasts by Rajani LaRocca Teen: The House of Saphir by Marissa Meyer

events.title: Teen/Tween Top Secret Book Club
events.description: Visit the teen area for your book/mission! Choose a teen or tween title. Read the book, decipher the codeword, and come back to the branch with the codeword to get a prize! Participation welcome at any point of the month. Titles will change at the beginning of each month. Ask a librarian for further details on titles and reading levels if desired.

events.title: 𓄿SHADOWBOX𓄿: the YA Fiction Trope Box
events.description: A new trope every month, a different story in every box. Each SHADOWBOX contains a YA Fiction book based on a shared theme or trope—like found family or enemies to lovers—and explore how each story brings it to life. Read, discover, and connect with other readers. Once registered, you'll receive an email notifying you that your SHADOWBOX is ready for pickup.This month's trope is: Asian Fantasy.

events.title: Teen Passive Book Club: June
events.description: The Teen Passive Book Club theme for June is Romance! Our featured title is Gwen & Art Are Not in Love by Lex Croucher: Hundreds of years since King Arthur’s reign, his descendant, Arthur, has been betrothed to Gwendoline since birth. The only thing they can agree on is that they despise each other. Grab a copy (or any alternate title) in the teen area & an activity packet!

events.title: Manga of the Month Club
events.description: Do you love manga, but hate in-person book discussions? Try our Manga of the Month Club! Pick up the first volume of the month's chosen series along with a review sheet. Return a completed review sheet to the Westwood Branch at any time and receive a small prize. This month's manga is Laid-back Camp! "Setting up a tent, starting a fire, and eating noodles with Mt. Fuji in view...Rin and Nadeshiko absolutely love to camp! Enjoy their zest for travel and maybe even pick up a tip or two about roughing it in the great outdoors. You might just want to go camping yourself!" -- Provided by publisher. This activity is recommended for ages 13-18 since the series are typically rated T for Teen.

events.description: Button Book Club is a passive book club, where participants read a book inspired by the monthly theme, fill in a brief worksheet (2 questions) and collect button. Something else spark your interest? We can't wait to hear what it is. Recommended for all ages. Please see staff if you have any questions.

events.title: Mayor's Summer Reading Club
events.description: The Mayor’s Summer Reading Club (MSRC) is a program for children ages birth to five and their families that exists to promote literacy and work against the summer reading slump. Every year, they announce a city-wide book choice for infants (ages 0 to 3) and children (ages 3-5) and partner with organizations like Fulton County Library to distribute copies of the books to children and families at no cost. Summer 2026's book is "Dinosaur!" by Olivia Aston Bosworth, about a little boy who loves dinosaurs and sees them in everything! Both English and Spanish language copies are available and will be until we run out.

events.title: Getting Cozy Book Club
events.description: If you've never read a cozy mystery before or if you have read thousands, join us each month as we read and discuss the first book in a series. We will meet on the 2nd Thursday of the month at 10 am. What is a cozy mystery? The crime-solver is an amateur sleuth, who eavesdrops, gathers clues, and uses their native intelligence and knowledge of their community to solve the crime. Cozy mysteries are generally considered “gentle” books and most have no graphic violence. The setting is often a small town full of colorful characters.Series frequently have a prominent thematic element introduced by the detective's job, pet, or hobby.These books are plot and character driven with an emphasis on solving puzzles rather than suspense. Our chosen book with serve as our theme for the meeting, we will talk about other book series with a similar theme as well as the recipes, crafts, and more included in many cozies.

Need sign language interpretation or other accessibility assistance for this event? Please email Nancy Novotny at NNovotny@omahalibrary.org to request accommodation. events.title: Adult Book Discussion: A Proposal They Can't Refuse by Natalie Caña
events.description: Join Austin Branch Library for a discussion of A Proposal They Can't Refuse by Natalie Caña. Kamilah Vega is desperate to convince her family to update their Puerto Rican restaurant and enter it into the Fall Foodie Tour. With the gentrification of their Chicago neighborhood, it's the only way to save the place. The fly in her mofongo—her blackmailing abuelo says if she wants to change anything in his restaurant, she'll have to marry the one man she can't stand: his best friend's grandson. Liam Kane spent a decade working to turn his family's distillery into a contender. But just as he and his grandfather are on the verge of winning a national competition, Granda hits him with a one-two punch: he has cancer and has his heart set on seeing Liam married before it's too late. And Granda knows just the girl...Kamilah Vega.Books are available at the circulation desk. AccessibilityNeed sign language interpretation or other accessibility assistance for this event? Please call (312) 747-8184 or email access@chipublib.org to request accommodations. Requests must be made at least 14 business days before the event.

events.title: High Intermediate - Advanced ESL Book Club - A Farewell to Arms
events.description: ESL Book Club will read A Farewell to Arms by Ernest Hemingway. This book club is best for high intermediate - advanced English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then you will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Fernando Lopez. Meeting dates: May 28; June 4, 11, 18, 25; July 2, 9, 16.Meeting time: Thursdays, 10:30am - 12:00nRegistration is open now! Book club is limited to 8 participants. Books will be provided for registered participants to keep. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting.A Farewell to Arms:Hemingway’s masterpiece is not only one of the best novels we have about World War I but is also a tender, haunting love story. Ernest Hemingway drew from his own war experiences when he crafted this remarkable story of an American ambulance driver serving on the Italian front and his love for a beautiful English nurse. The novel, Hemingway’s first best seller, is marked by vivid depictions of the horrors of the battlefield—but also by the heartrending vicissitudes of a passionate affair of the heart between his protagonists, Frederic and Catherine, leading up to a tragic ending that is all the more powerful for its famously understated expression.

events.title: Civic Center Morning Mystery Book Group: 'Past Lying' by Val McDermid
events.description: As part of our celebration and commemoration of Pride in June, the Civic Center Library's Morning Mystery Book Group will meet on Thursday, June 11th from 11:00am-12:00pm to discuss Past Lying by Val McDermid, a pioneering figure in queer literature whose debut novel in 1987 featured the first British lesbian detective; notably, McDermid continues to feature LGBTQ+ characters in her novels. This mystery book group generally meets on the 2nd Thursday of every month from 11:00-12:00n. All are welcome to join! Request a copy of the book online or ask at another County Library location for assistance in placing a hold Past Lying (book) or Past Lying (eBook) by Val McDermid 7th book in the Scotland Detective Chief Inspector Karen Pirie series. It’s April 2020 and Edinburgh is in lockdown. It would seem like a strange time for a cold case to go hot—the streets all but empty, an hour’s outdoor exercise the maximum allowed—but a mere pandemic doesn’t mean crime takes a holiday. When a source at the National Library contacts DCI Karen Pirie’s team about documents in the archive of a recently deceased crime novelist, it seems it’s game on again. At the center of it, a novel: two crime novelists facing off over a chessboard. But it quickly emerges that their real-life competition is drawing blood. What unspools is a twisted game of betrayal and revenge, and as Karen and her team attempt to disentangle fact from fiction, it becomes clear that their investigation is more complicated than they ever imagined. ADA Accommodation Request | Solicitud de Adaptación ADA

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: Miss Kopp's Midnight Confessions
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Join us to share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read.Miss Kopp's Midnight Confessions by Amy Stewart SYNOPSIS: Deputy sheriff Constance Kopp is outraged to see young women brought into the Hackensack jail over dubious charges of waywardness, incorrigibility, and moral depravity. Constance uses her authority as deputy sheriff, and occasionally exceeds it, to investigate and defend these women when no one else will. But it's her sister Fleurette who puts Constance's beliefs to the test and forces her to reckon with her own ideas of how a young woman should and shouldn't behave.

events.title: June Moraga Book Club
events.description: Join other book lovers for a lively discussion regarding Elizabeth Lett's, The Ride of Her Life: "The true story of Annie Wilkins, a 63-year-old Maine farmer who, after losing her farm and being diagnosed with a terminal illness, embarked on a 4,000-mile horseback journey across America in 1954 to see the Pacific Ocean. Accompanied by her horse, Tarzan, and her dog, Depeche Toi, she navigated the changing landscape of the 1950s, relying on the kindness of strangers and her own grit to complete her last-chance adventure."Note that Moraga Book Club is for adults ages 18 and up.

events.description: Teen Book Club has transformed into Read It and Eat! It's a book club with a twist--during book club, you create something yummy based on this month's theme and then feast. This month, the theme is Book Fair and we will be making nachos! What is a book fair party? Just like a school book fair, we will have books and bookish swag. How do you get it? By earning Book Bucks. Play games, make crafts, logging books in the Summer Reading Challenge, and more to earn Book Bucks to redeem for prizes including a special auction at the end of the party. Readers of manga, graphic novels, books, and audiobooks are welcome! Even if you haven't read anything new lately, you can still join us! This program is for readers ages 13-18.

events.title: Books Alive: The Ballerina With Webbed Feet
events.description: Come see Books Alive bring the children's book The Ballerina with Webbed Feet! from the page to the stage in a fully-staged original musical play that your family will love! Join us on Thursday, June 11th at 3:00 pm for Books Alive: The Ballerina with Webbed Feet!Books Alive adapts children's literature into fully-staged original 45 minute musicals, carefully honoring the author’s message, encouraging students to develop a healthy love of books, while often providing their first opportunity to see live theatre. Crafted and performed by a professional troupe, productions are bright, engaging, fun, and entertaining for all ages!"Pattie Patty is head over webbed feet in love with ballet! But seriously, can a duck even dance? What’s the secret sauce to becoming a prima ballerina? Join her on a quest to discover that it involves more than just wishing! Will she ever manage a pirouette without a belly flop? Will she find a way to be happy with her feathery self? Pattie learns that ballet isn’t just about dreaming big; it takes some flapping! While the Gabi Goose is rolling in the pond with laughter and Sarge the Frog is turning red with embarrassment, Pattie finds out who her true pals really are."This is one of our Thursday Summer Fun Kids Events at the Fairbanks Library. It is designed for elementary school-aged children and their families. Tickets are required for this event and all Thursday Summer Fun Kids Events. Tickets are FREE and available at the Fairbanks Library service desk starting at 2:30 pm on the day of the event. Tickets are limited and offered on a first-come, first-served basis. Individuals must be present inside the library to receive a ticket, and everyone attending the program must have a ticket to be admitted. All children must be accompanied by an adult, and adults must remain with their child(ren) during the program.Our Thursday Summer Fun Kids Events are ticketed to ensure the safety of our program attendees and the requirements of our presenters.All Thursday Summer Fun Kids Events will be held at the Houston CyFair Lions Club located across the parking lot from the library. Remember to get your tickets inside the library first! We will start seating attendees at 2:45 pm with the program starting at 3:00 pm. Hope to see you there!!This program is provided in part by the generous support of the Friends of the Fairbanks Library. Programs and services are Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Compliant. Special accommodations can be requested in advance by contacting the library.

events.title: Medicare 101
events.description: New to Medicare? Enrolled but still confused? Join us to learn: Medicare Parts A, B and D Medicare Advantage plans (Part C) Medicare Supplement plans (medigaps) Your own coverage options and enrollment deadlines What’s New in 2026 Our HICAP of San Mateo County (Health Insurance Counseling and Advocacy Program) counselors will explain the many facets of Medicare and provide an overview of different coverage options and will share how HICAP can help you navigate the many decisions you need to make regarding Medicare. HICAP is a state and federally funded program that provides free, unbiased counseling and community education on Medicare and related health insurance issues. HICAP does not sell, endorse, or recommend any specific health insurance. HICAP counselors are registered by the California Department of Aging and provide confidential one-on-one counseling appointments both in person and over the phone.

events.title: The Scaffolding Method: Drafting Enduring Historical Fiction (Virtual Program)
events.description: Author Lindsay Marie Morris wasn't always a "plotter," but when she secured a two-book contract with just six months to submit, she needed a system that balanced historical rigor with rapid drafting. The result was the Scaffolding Method, a framework designed to bridge the gaps in the factual record and bring the human experience to life. Lindsay will show writers at all stages how to build a narrative scaffold step-by-step using the four core components of her signature approach: The Standard: Constructing a timeline that prioritizes oral histories and primary archives to anchor your story in truth.The Ledger: Using narrative economy and recurring objects to stabilize your plot through cause and effect.The Platform: Staging pivotal scenes that layer in cultural and social context.The Brace: Strengthening emotional architecture by tying personal stakes to historical risks. Learn how to use these tools to perform a "stress test" on each draft, ensuring your story never sways or collapses. Lindsay will also provide tips for applying these techniques to other kinds of fiction writing. This program will be presented virtually via Zoom and will be recorded. Registration is required, and closes 24 hours before the event begins. A link to the Zoom meeting and a handout will be emailed to registrants the evening before the program. About the Speaker Lindsay Marie Morris is a novelist and journalist based in Los Angeles. Inspired by her Sicilian-American roots, her debut novel, The Last Letter from Sicily (Storm Publishing, January 2025), traces a World War II–era story of love, resilience, and family secrets that spans continents and generations. Her second novel, Beneath the Sicilian Stars (Storm Publishing, July 2025), returns to this pivotal moment in history, following a family divided between California and Sicily whose lives are forever changed by the war, forcing them to confront questions of loyalty, belonging, and sacrifice.A graduate of Marquette University, Lindsay began her career in magazine publishing, serving as an editor at publications including Folio: and Shape, before expanding into digital media, nonprofit communications, and freelance journalism.Lindsay coaches writers, visits book clubs, and is a frequent speaker and educator for the Osher Lifelong Learning Institute, public libraries, historical museums, and cultural organizations. events.title: Book Buzz: Not Quite Dead Yet
events.description: Join us to discuss Not Quite Dead Yet by Holly Jackson. --- Copies of the book will be reserved and available at the library and electronic versions can be found on Hoopla or Libby.Registration for this event is encouraged but not required.In seven days Jet Mason will be dead. Jet is the daughter of one of the wealthiest families in Woodstock, Vermont. Twenty-seven years old, she's still waiting for her life to begin. I'll do it later, she always says. She has time. Until Halloween night, when Jet is violently attacked by an unseen intruder. She suffers a catastrophic head injury. The doctor is certain that within a week, the injury will trigger a deadly aneurysm. Jet has never thought of herself as having enemies. But now she looks at everyone in a new light: her family, her former best friend turned sister-in-law, her ex-boyfriend. She has at most seven days, and as her condition deteriorates she has only her childhood friend Billy for help. But nevertheless, she's absolutely determined to finally finish something: Jet is going to solve her own murder.

events.title: Adult Book Discussion: First Lie Wins
events.description: Join the Garfield Ridge Branch for a discussion of First Lie Wins by Ashley Elston "Evie Porter has everything a nice, Southern girl could want: a perfect, doting boyfriend, a house with a white picket fence and a garden, a fancy group of friends. The only catch: Evie Porter doesn't exist. The identity comes first: Evie Porter. Once she's given a name and location by her mysterious boss Mr. Smith, she learns everything there is to know about the town and the people in it. Then the mark: Ryan Sumner. The last piece of the puzzle is the job. Evie isn't privy to Mr. Smith's real identity, but she knows this job will be different. Ryan has gotten under her skin, and she's starting to envision a different sort of life for herself. But Evie can't make any mistakes--especially after what happened last time. Because the one thing she's worked her entire life to keep clean, the one identity she could always go back to-her real identity-just walked right into this town. Evie Porter must stay one step ahead of her past while making sure there's still a future in front of her. The stakes couldn't be higher--but then, Evie has always liked a challenge.." events.title: Virtual Author Talk: Matt Dinniman
events.description: Matt Dinniman, New York Times bestselling author of the Dungeon Crawler Carl series, joins us to discuss his newest installment, A Parade of Horribles. In this chapter of Carl and Donut’s journey, chaos spreads outside the dungeon while the pair face a deceptively straightforward set of races on the tenth floor, choosing upgrades and navigating increasingly difficult tracks as strange glitches and ominous rumors about the eleventh floor grow harder to ignore. Although everything appears routine, Carl senses that something is deeply wrong and refuses to accept the limits placed on him. As the system AI hints at a mysterious “coming-out party for the ages,” Carl prepares a dangerous plan of his own, one that he cannot share with anyone and that carries the highest stakes yet for both him and Donut. ---The Santa Clara County Library District is proud to present a free virtual author‑talk series featuring bestselling, award‑winning, and acclaimed writers from around the world, offering live Q&A sessions two to three times a month. Event links are sent about 24 hours in advance (or upon late registration), recordings are available for a limited time, and a full list of upcoming programs can be found here. ---For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+ days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.

It is a prediction that sparks her life’s work—trying to solve a crime that hasn’t happened yet."— Copies of the title are available at the circulation desk. Registration is suggested. events.title: Book Discussion: On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous by Ocean Vuong
events.description: Join Albany Park Library for a discussion of On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous by Ocean Vuong. On Earth We’re Briefly Gorgeous is a letter from a son to a mother who cannot read. Written when the speaker, Little Dog, is in his late twenties, the letter unearths a family’s history that began before he was born — a history whose epicenter is rooted in Vietnam — and serves as a doorway into parts of his life his mother has never known, all of it leading to an unforgettable revelation.At once a witness to the fraught yet undeniable love between a single mother and her son, it is also a brutally honest exploration of race, class, and masculinity. Asking questions central to our American moment, immersed as we are in addiction, violence, and trauma, but undergirded by compassion and tenderness, On Earth We’re Briefly Gorgeous is as much about the power of telling one’s own story as it is about the obliterating silence of not being heard.Copies are available at the Reference Desk. AccessibilityNeed sign language interpretation or other accessibility assistance for this event? Please call (312) 747-8184 or email access@chipublib.org to request accommodations. Requests must be made at least 14 business days before the event.

events.title: Xenia Community Book Club
events.description: Join us to discuss The Correspondent by Virginia Evans . Everyone is welcome! --- Copies of the book are available at the information desk at your Xenia Community Library. If you prefer an eBook or eAudiobook, check Libby or Hoopla. About the book:"Sybil is seventy-three years old, in the winter of her life. Sybil has always made sense of the world through writing letters and through this epistolary novel we see how she comes to terms with her past and present and learns forgiveness"--Throughout her life Sybil Van Antwerp has used letters to make sense of the world and her place in it. Most mornings around half past ten Sybil sits down to write letters--to her brother, to her best friend, to the president of the university who will not allow her to audit a class she desperately wants to take, to Joan Didion and Larry McMurtry to tell them what she thinks of their latest books, and to one person to whom she writes often yet never sends the letter. Sybil expects her world to go on as it always has. A mother, grandmother, wife, divorče, distinguished lawyer, she has lived a full life. But when letters from someone in her past force her to examine one of the most painful periods of her life, she realizes the letter she has been writing over the years needs to be read and that she cannot move forward until she finds it in her heart to offer forgiveness.

events.title: Graphic Novel Book Club: First Test
events.description: Join other readers to discuss a different graphic novel each month. Ages 10-14. Registration required. --- This month we're reading First Test by Tamora Pierce. "This eagerly awaited graphic novel adaptation of the iconic fantasy novel First Test reimagines the classic knight's tale, but with a twist. In a world not quite ready for her ambition, a determined girl seeks knighthood and finds herself on a mission to prove it. Keladry is the first girl who dares to take advantage of a new rule in her homeland, Tortall— one that allows women to train for knighthood. She knows she's ready for the traditional hazing and grueling schedule of a page, but standing in her way is the knights' training master, Lord Wyldon. The training master is dead set against girls becoming knights, and in an attempt to further hold Kel back, Lord Wyldon decides that she must pass a one-year trial that no male page has ever had to endure. But she is not to be underestimated. She will fight to succeed, even if the test is purposely unfair. The Protector of the Small series gets its first graphic novel adaptation, bringing to life Tamora Pierce's bestselling First Test in an exciting new format. This graphic novel story about believing in yourself and overcoming all odds combines action and adventure in a way that new and old fans of the series will love."

events.title: Chicago History Book Club: SDS and The Weather Underground
events.description: The Students for a Democratic Society (SDS) was formed in 1960 to bring about social and economic justice by educating college students and urban community members in non-violent political activism. When opposition to the Vietnam War became heated, major splits over goals and the use of violent tactics emerged in the organization. At a national SDS convention in Chicago in 1969, a faction later known as the Weather Underground took over the leadership of SDS and shut it down. Pick from several book titles available for the discussion of these two groups. Books will be available at the first floor circulation desk. This program is in partnership with the Edgewater Historical Society. Accessibility Need sign language interpretation or other accessibility assistance for this event? Please call (312) 747-8184 or email access@chipublib.org to request accommodations. Requests must be made at least 14 business days before the event.

events.title: Never 2 Old 4 YA Book Club: This Ravenous Fate
events.description: Do you like reading YA fiction as an adult? Then come join Kelly’s Never 2 Old 4 YA Book Club. We meet on the second Saturday of every month. We will be reading and discussing This Ravenous Fate by Hayley Dennings. Set in 1926 New York, Elise Saint, a reluctant heir to her family empire of vampire hunters, and a young reaper named Layla Quinn are forced to work together to investigate the attacks that may lead to a reaper cure. Once close friends, now bitter enemies, Elise and Layla explore the city's underworld, confronting their intense feelings for one another and uncovering the sinister truths about a growing threat to reapers and humans alike. You can pick up a copy of the book at Kelly branch or your local library. Refreshments will be provided. Disclaimer: Ages 16 and up can join. This is a teen/adult emerging program to bridge a gap between teens and adults' services.AccessibilityNeed sign language interpretation or other accessibility assistance for this event? events.description: Join us for Reading Came First, a book club that explores the powerful connections between literature, music, identity, and culture. This monthly gathering creates space for thoughtful conversation about books that challenge, inspire, and reflect the world around us. Each month we’ll read and discuss works by groundbreaking authors across fiction, nonfiction, poetry, and cultural criticism. Together we’ll explore themes like storytelling, community, creativity, and social change while connecting literature to music, art, and lived experience. Whether you’re an avid reader or just looking to join a welcoming community conversation, all are welcome.Accessibility Need sign language interpretation or other accessibility assistance for this event? events.title: Between the Sheets: A Romance Book Club
events.description: If you love talking tropes, subgenres, HEA versus HFN then this is the book club for you. Every month we'll read a different romance book and come together to discuss what we loved or hated. Books will range from closed door to explicit and explore a wide variety of subgenres.This month's title is A Gentleman's Gentleman by TJ Alexander.The notoriously eccentric Lord Christopher Eden is a "man of unusual make" and even more unusual habits: he wears pastels year round, prefers to live as far from the prying eyes and ears of the ton as possible, and wholeheartedly prefers the comfortable company of his childhood cook and aged butler, Plinkton, to any swarm of servants that would normally befit a man of his station. His penchant for privacy makes for a pleasant, if occasionally lonely life-one that threatens to be upended entirely when Christopher receives word from his lawyers that, according to his late father's will, he must find a wife in London by the end of the Season if he intends to maintain his status as the only living heir to the Eden's End estate. While most men his age and status would leap at the chance to marry, he cannot imagine a worse fate. After all, as a "man of unusual make" who also doesn't happen to be attracted to women, his chances of making a wife happy are about on par with his future wife's chances of staying silent about their arrangement. Enter the handsome-if stoic-James Harding, the new valet Christopher very reluctantly hires after Cook and Plinkton remind him that if he's to stay in London, he must keep up appearances befitting that of a wealthy, eligible bachelor. After a rocky start to their relationship (wherein Harding simply does not understand why Christopher won't let him dress him, wait on him, or fulfill any other typical valet duties, for that matter), the two strike up a fragile friendship amid the throes of the London Season-a friendship that threatens to shatter completely as Christopher's deadline to find a wife looms, and Harding reveals that he's been harboring a rather consequential secret of his own. With its heady combination of dry wit, slow-burn romance, and subtle commentary on the complexity of trans identity and relationships that's as relevant now as it was during the Regency era, Eden's End stands to transform the historical romance genre as we know it Steam Level: 🔥🔥🔥🔥4/5 DML welcomes everyone to our programs. If you have questions or need any accommodations, please contact us so we can help make the program a good experience for you. Check out our social story to familiarize yourself with the Miami Township Branch!

events.title: Adult Book Club: Tom Lake
events.description: Please join us for a discussion of Tom Lake by Ann Patchett. In the spring of 2020, Lara's three daughters return to the family's orchard in Northern Michigan. While picking cherries, they beg their mother to tell them the story of Peter Duke, a famous actor with whom she shared both a stage and a romance years before at a theater company called Tom Lake. As Lara recalls the past, her daughters examine their own lives and relationship with their mother, and are forced to reconsider the world and everything they thought they knew.Place a hold or pick up a copy at the Avalon circulation desk. How to AttendThis event takes place in person. Questions about visiting the library? Check out our Using the Library FAQs. events.title: Pineville Rainbow Reads Teen Book Club
events.description: This club meets monthly to discuss titles that feature LGBTQIA+ writers and characters. This program is provided by Pineville Library Staff and recommended for teens ages 14 to 18 and new adults 18-24. Information about this title:Evan Horowitz has it all: beauty, brains, and a not-so-secret flair for matchmaking! An Insta influencer with a talent for makeup and a taste for romance, he’s no stranger to playing cupid for those hopelessly clueless in finding love.So when shy transfer student Natalia shuffles into school one day, Evan can’t help but get his hands messy! With so many matches to choose from, it’s not long before he sets a plan in motion for Natalia—much against the better judgement of his level-headed best friend, Davi.But he takes things too far, creating a web of drama that spirals out of his control. Can Evan learn to put the people closest to him before his misguided ambition? Or will he lose them and his own chance at romance, too?Looking for the book? Check out our catalog and place a hold:Book: Mismatched How to Join an Online Program This event takes place on Zoom. You’ll receive an email with a link to the secure Zoom meeting with a confirmation email from staff. New to using Zoom? Check out these resources to help you get started.

Club"María Mínguez Arias, an essayist, translator, editor, and author of "Nombrar el cuerpo", "Patricia sigue aquí", and "Naming the Body: A Queer Woman's Restorative Mapping of the Self"MariNaomi, a graphic novelist, cartoonist, and author of "I Thought You Loved Me", "Dirty Produce", the Life on Earth series, "I Thought YOU Hated ME", "Turning Japanese", "Dragon’s Breath and Other True Stories", and more!Nidhi Chanani, an illustrator, speaker, and author of "Pashmina", "Super Boba Cafe", "Shark Princess", and illustrator of "I Will Be Fierce!", "Strong", and more! events.description: Join the Avalon Cookbook Club to explore delicious food and recipes from around the world. This month we celebrate Juneteenth. This month the selections are: Watermelon & Red Birds - A Cookbook for Juneteenth and Black Celebrations The first cookbook dedicated to Juneteenth, blending traditional African American foodways with modern flair through over 75 recipes, essays, and resources for celebrating Black freedom and joy. Jubilee- Recipes from Two Centuries of African American Cooking Adapted from historical texts and rare African American cookbooks, the 125 recipes of Jubilee paint a rich, varied picture of the true history of African American cooking a cuisine far beyond soul food. Try out recipes and we’ll talk about them. If you'd like to bring a sample from the book that’s great but not required. events.title: Adult Book Discussion: Beloved
events.description: Join us for a discussion of Beloved by Toni Morrison. Winner of the Pulitzer Prize, Toni Morrison's Beloved is a spellbinding and dazzlingly innovative portrait of a woman haunted by the past. Sethe was born a slave and escaped to Ohio, but eighteen years later she is still not free. She has borne the unthinkable and not gone mad, yet she is still held captive by memories of Sweet Home, the beautiful farm where so many hideous things happened. Meanwhile Sethe's house has long been troubled by the angry, destructive ghost of her baby, who died nameless and whose tombstone is engraved with a single word: Beloved. A limited number of copies are available at the branch. AccessibilityNeed sign language interpretation or other accessibility assistance for this event? Please call (312) 747-8184 or email access@chipublib.org to request accommodations. Requests must be made at least 14 business days before the event.

For our June meeting, we’re reading Another Kind by by Trevor Bream and Cait May."Tucked away in a government facility nicknamed the Playroom, six not-quite-human kids learn to control their strange and unpredictable abilities. Life is good—or safe, at least—hidden from the prying eyes of a judgmental world. That is, until a security breach forces them out of their home and into the path of the Collector, a mysterious being with leech-like powers. Can the group band together to thwart the Collector’s devious plan, or will they wind up the newest addition to his collection?" You can find a copy through our catalog or check one out at the front desk, while copies are available!

events.title: Nonfiction Book Discussion: Challenger:A True Story of Heroism and Disaster on the Edge of Space
events.description: Open to all adults who love to read and discuss non-fictional literature. In June the Cupertino Library Nonfiction Book Discussion Group will talk about Challenger: A True Story of Heroism and Disaster on the Edge of Space by Adam Higginbotham. (2024) Adam Higginbotham’s account of the Challenger disaster of January 1986 follows the lives of the seven crew members aboard the Challenger, including teacher Christa McAuliffe. Famously and tragically, Challenger broke apart in mid air over the Atlantic Ocean seconds after its launch, leaving no survivors. While the nation was given ‘feel good” stories about Challenger’s mission in years and months leading up to the launch, funding was trimmed at the expense of safety by politicians, and warning signs were recognized, but often ignored or concealed from the public. When the investigation was underway, leakers and whistleblowers came forward, determined to reveal the truth about how the disaster came about. The nonfiction book discussion group meets on the second Sunday of each month at 11am in Room 201B. All meetings are sponsored by The Friends of the Cupertino Library. For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@sccl.org.

This session, we’ll be reading Almost American Girl, Everything We Never Had, and Scythe—exploring themes of identity, family, and the choices that shape our lives. Whether you’re an avid reader or just getting started, this is a welcoming space to share perspectives, think critically, and connect with other teens. events.description: Come make your very own Chicago comic! In this fun, one‑hour program, kids draw pictures, make a character and tell a short story about themselves and the neighborhood where they live. Parents and caregivers join the fun - read, draw, write and create comic books together to win prizes. No special writing or drawing skills needed - just imagination and teamwork. For children 5 and up with their caregivers. How To AttendRegister by the start of the program. Chicago Public Library cannot collect personal information online from kids 0 to 13. A parent or guardian’s email address must be used to register. As a reminder, school email addresses may block external emails such as those from CPL. AccessibilityNeed sign language interpretation or other accessibility assistance for this event? Please call (312) 747-8184 or email access@chipublib.org to request accommodations. Requests must be made at least 14 business days before the event.

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: The Remains of the Day
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Join our book club for spirited discussions on a wide range of fiction and non-fiction. Each month we'll tackle a new title. Whether you loved it, loathed it, or left it unfinished, we want to hear your take. Come share your impressions and connect with fellow readers! This week we will be reading The Remains of the Day by Kazuo Ishiguro. “Here is Kazuo Ishiguro's profoundly compelling portrait of Stevens, the perfect butler, and of his fading, insular world in post-World War II England. Stevens, at the end of three decades of service at Darlington Hall, spending a day on a country drive, embarks as well on a journey through the past in an effort to reassure himself that he has served humanity by serving the "great gentleman," Lord Darlington. But lurking in his memory are doubts about the true nature of Lord Darlington's "greatness," and much graver doubts about the nature of his own life."

events.title: BeTWEEN the Pages
events.description: Join us in the extraordinary world of Graphic Novels with BeTWEEN the Pages, a book club for ages 8-12! This month's read will be Sword of the Champion by Eric Lide and can be found in our catalog here!Orin’s life is uprooted when he discovers that he is the reincarnation of a great hero, destined to defeat the evil Fiendlord. And he just may have a shot because, in the years since he conquered the world, the Fiendlord has become lazy and complacent. He sees Orin’s appearance as motivation to finally get back in shape and relive his glory years, and hopefully regain the respect of his unimpressed teenage daughter. In need of a mystical sword if he’s to stand a chance against the Fiendlord, Orin teams up with a precocious, entrepreneurial sorceress who's ready to launch Orin’s career as the Champion of Draeland—which would be much easier if he didn’t keep befriending the Fiends they’re supposed to destroy.Registration Required to attend. After you have registered, you may pick up your copy of the book at the front desk.

events.title: Black Lit Discussion Club (Cherish Farrah by Bethany C. Morrow)
events.description: Join us at the Black Lit Discussion Club, a vibrant and inclusive space dedicated to exploring and celebrating Black literature. Whether you're a lifelong reader or new to the genre, our club welcomes everyone who is passionate about diving into the rich and diverse world of Black authors and stories. Each month, we select a book that highlights different aspects of the Black experience, spanning various genres and time periods. Our discussions are engaging, thoughtful, and respectful, fostering a community where every voice is heard and valued. Come and share your insights, discover new perspectives, and connect with fellow literature enthusiasts. Let's celebrate the power of Black voices together!

events.description: Lit Happens is a book club for twenty- and thirty-somethings in the Plaza Midwood/NoDa area who love good stories, good vibes, and good coffee. We read across genres, meet monthly, and never judge anyone for choosing the audiobook. Come for the books, stay for the banter. The title for this month is The Song of Achilles by Madeline Miller. Looking for the book? Find it in our catalog here. This program will be provided by Maggie at the Plaza Midwood Library. This book club will plan to meet on the last Monday of every month at the Qahwah House in Plaza Midwood at 1318 Pecan Ave in Charlotte, NC. There may be limited parking at the venue, participants are welcome to park at the Plaza Midwood library and walk. Accessibility Statement For in-person programs: Charlotte Mecklenburg Library is committed to providing accessible programs and events. This program will be offered in person at a library location. To request an accommodation, please contact us by phone or email at least one week prior to the program. Requests can be submitted by calling (704) 416-6200 or emailing malbert@cmlibrary.org.

events.title: Readability Book Group for Adults with Disabilities
events.description: The Readability Book Group is a group for adults with disabilities that is run in partnership with Center for Independent Futures. We meet weekly on Mondays via Zoom. We read a new book (or books!) every 3 months. Our sessions run from January-March, April-June, July-September and October-December. Each meeting, we will recap what we have read so far, then the facilitator will read the next part of the book aloud. Afterwards, we will discuss what we just read. Our book for April-June will be: THE BOOK WITCH by Meg Shaffer. Registration can be done here on the library website or via Center for Independent Futures. Note: There will be no book group on May 25th

events.title: Camano Island Library Online Book Group - "The Measure"
events.description: Join us for a Hybrid Book Group at the Camano Island Library. During this session, we will be discussing The Measure by Nikki Erlick. One day eight people open their fronts door, to find a small wooden box. The contents of this mysterious box tells them the exact number of years you will live. As society comes together and pulls apart, everyone faces the same shocking choice: Do they wish to know how long they’ll live? And, if so, what will they do with that knowledge? This compelling book club fiction explores that very question. A limited number of copies of the book will be available at the Camano Island Library.This is a hybrid event, meaning you can attend either online or in-person. Registration is required to receive a reminder email with the Zoom link 24 hours before the event begins. If you register less than 24 hours before the event, your confirmation email will have the Zoom link. If you're attending in person, you do not need to register for the event. Online Events FAQs Contact UsTo request a sign language interpreter, complete this online form or contact your local library at least two weeks prior to the library event. Interpreters will be provided based on availability.

events.title: Strange New Worlds
events.description: Join us usually, but not always, on the third Monday of each month at 7:00 pm for the Strange New Worlds book club! This month's book is All the Water in the World by Eiren Caffall. From the publisher: "In the years after the glaciers melt, Nonie, her older sister and her parents and their researcher friends have stayed behind in an almost deserted New York City, creating a settlement on the roof of the American Museum of Natural History. The rule: Take from the exhibits only in dire need. They hunt and grow their food in Central Park as they work to save the collections of human history and science. When a superstorm breaches the city's flood walls, Nonie and her family must escape north on the Hudson. They carry with them a book that holds their records of the lost collections. Racing on the swollen river towards what may be safety, they encounter communities that have adapted in very different and sometimes frightening ways to the new reality. But they are determined to find a way to make a new world that honors all they've saved. Inspired by the stories of the curators in Iraq and Leningrad who worked to protect their collections from war, All the Water in the World is both a meditation on what we save from collapse and an adventure story-with danger, storms, and a fight for survival. In the spirit of From the Mixed-Up Files of Mrs. Basil E. Frankweiler and Parable of the Sower, this wild journey offers the hope that what matters most - love and work, community and knowledge--will survive" This program will be provided by Charlotte Mecklenburg Library staff and our wonderful Volunteer leader. Information about this Group The Strange New Worlds Book Club meets on the 3rd Monday of the month to discuss Science Fiction and Fantasy titles. How to Join an Online ProgramThis event will take place Zoom and in person. Please call 704-416-4000 for more information. Looking for more technology resources? Check out this list!

Listen via the link or on Apple or Spotify. Here's what to listen to before we meet: June 15: Episodes 1 and 2June 29: Episodes 3 and 4July 13: Episodes 5-7This program is for adults 18 and up due to mature themes, violence, and adult language that may be in the podcasts. These podcasts may contain triggering themes and may feature content warnings. This program will be provided by North County and Matthews library staff. How to Join an Online Program This event takes place on Zoom. Please register by 5:00 p.m. the day before the event. events.title: Talk It Up! Book Club
events.description: Join the Talk It Up! book club in June as we read and discuss The Last Flight by Julie Clarke. "Claire Cook has a perfect life. Married to the scion of a political dynasty, with a Manhattan townhouse and a staff of ten, her surroundings are elegant, her days flawlessly choreographed, and her future auspicious. But behind closed doors, nothing is quite as it seems. That perfect husband has a temper that burns as bright as his promising political career, and he's not above using his staff to track Claire's every move, making sure she's living up to his impossible standards. But what he doesn't know is that Claire has worked for months on a plan to vanish. A chance meeting in an airport bar brings her together with a woman whose circumstances seem equally dire. Together they make a last-minute decision to switch tickets -- Claire taking Eva's flight to Oakland, and Eva traveling to Puerto Rico as Claire. They believe the swap will give each of them the head start they need to begin again somewhere far away. But when the flight to Puerto Rico goes down, Claire realizes it's no longer a head start but a new life. Cut off, out of options, with the news of her death about to explode in the media, Claire will assume Eva's identity, and along with it, the secrets Eva fought so hard to keep hidden."A limited number of copies of the book selection are available to check out at the downstairs circulation desk.

events.title: 星期一下午:三国演义 (儿童读书会)
events.description: A book club dedicated to "Three Kingdoms," the classic Chinese fiction. This club is designed for children who are fans of the story and is conducted entirely in Mandarin Chinese. The event is organized by dedicated teen volunteers from Lynbrook High School.ADA Accommodation Requests小朋友们，你们听说过《三国演义》吗？很久很久以前，在中国古代，有三个非常厉害的国家：魏国、蜀国和吴国。那个时代英雄特别多，就像你最喜欢的超级英雄电影一样！只要我们像他们一样勇敢、聪明，并且讲信用，就能克服生活中的很多困难！ 这里有几个你一定会喜欢的精彩小故事, 还有名的成语故事: 三英战吕布, 王统巧用连环计, 曹操挟天子以令诸侯, 官渡之战, 煮酒论英雄, 千里走单骑, 三顾茅庐, 舌战群儒, 草船借箭, 苦肉计, 赤壁之战, 三气周瑜, 单刀赴会, 智取定军山, 水淹七军, 大意失荆州, 败走麦城, 火烧连营, 七擒孟获, 失街亭, 空城计, 星落五丈原, 九伐中原, 三家归晋. 免费的亲子中文活動. 座位数量有限. 门票将在故事时间开始前 30 分钟在询问处领取。 圖書館中文资源: 大人最新中文书, 中文電子書, 非小說類電子書 (Overdrive) 有歡迎大家一起來借閱電子書. Overdirve平台: 小朋友的中文電子書, 大朋友的中文電子小說, 大朋友的非小說類的電子書籍. Hoopla平台: 中文有聲書籍 美国残疾人法案(ADA)申请提供协助 7/20/2026

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: Anxious People
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Come and share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read. This week we will be reading Anxious People by Fredrik Backman.An apartment open house becomes a life-or-death situation when a failed bank robber bursts in and takes eight extremely anxious strangers hostage. The captives include a retired couple who hunt down fixer-uppers to avoid the painful truth that they can't fix up their own marriage, a wealthy banker who has been too busy making money to care about anyone else, a young couple who are about to have their first baby but can't seem to agree on anything, an eighty-year old woman who has lived long enough not to be afraid of someone waving a gun in her face, a flustered, but still-ready-to-make-a-deal real estate agent, and a mystery man who has locked himself in the apartment's only bathroom.

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: Frankenstein
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. We'll feature different titles and authors each month. Join us to share your impressions, thoughts and interpretations of a book we have all read. Frankenstein by Mary ShelleyMary Shelley's chilling Gothic tale was conceived when she was only eighteen, living with her lover Percy Shelley on Lake Geneva. The story of Victor Frankenstein who, obsessed with creating life itself, plunders graveyards for the material to fashion a new being, but whose botched creature sets out to destroy his maker, would become the world's most famous work of horror fiction, and remains a devastating exploration of the limits of human creativity.

events.title: Manchester Book Group
events.description: Pick up the next book club selection at the Manchester library service desk following the previous month's meeting on the 3rd Monday of the month. **NOTE Meetings in January and February each year are held on the FOURTH Monday of the month due to the Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day and President's Day holidays This month we are discussing:The Familiar by Leigh BardugoFrom the Publisher:In a shabby house, on a shabby street, in the new capital of Madrid, Luzia Cotado uses scraps of magic to get through her days of endless toil as a scullion. But when her scheming mistress discovers the lump of a servant cowering in the kitchen is actually hiding a talent for little miracles, she demands Luzia use those gifts to improve the family's social position.What begins as simple amusement for the nobility takes a perilous turn when Luzia garners the notice of Antonio Pérez, the disgraced secretary to Spain's king. Still reeling from the defeat of his armada, the king is desperate for any advantage in the war against England's heretic queen—and Pérez will stop at nothing to regain the king's favor.Determined to seize this one chance to better her fortunes, Luzia plunges into a world of seers and alchemists, holy men and hucksters, where the lines between magic, science, and fraud are never certain. But as her notoriety grows, so does the danger that her Jewish blood will doom her to the Inquisition's wrath. She will have to use every bit of her wit and will to survive—even if that means enlisting the help of Guillén Santángel, an embittered immortal familiar whose own secrets could prove deadly for them both.

All supplies provided - just bring your imagination! Every Tuesday morning, enjoy a relaxed, welcoming program featuring activities, games, and occasional guest visits - or simply unwind with a coffee or tea and connect with others. Large-print books and easy recipes will also be available for browsing. All abilities are welcome, and participation is always optional. Come for what interests you and stay as long as you like.

events.title: Brewed Awakenings Book Club
events.library: Public Library of Youngstown and Mahoning County
events.description: Tuesdays are better with books and coffee! The Brewed Awakening Book Club is your midweek escape to a cozy corner where you can enjoy a quiet morning with a good read and a hot cup of coffee. It’s the perfect way to slow down and recharge before the hustle of the week. Bring your own book, or check out a small selection of ours!

events.title: The Detection Club
events.description: Join us on the 3rd Tuesday of each month at 10:00 am on Zoom for The Detection Book Club! This month's selection is Welcome to Murder Week by Karen Dukess. From Library Journal: "After Cath's estranged mother dies and Cath is going through her things, she finds a ticket for a murder-mystery week in England. Baffled but intrigued, she decides to go. All the residents in a Peak District town come together to stage a fake murder in hopes of attracting tourism. Cath eagerly teams up with her cottagemates--Wyatt, who works unhappily in his husband's birding shop, and Amity, a divorced romance writer who is hoping to gain inspiration for her next book. As they gather clues and interview suspects, Cath also learns shocking and potentially life-altering truths about her mother and has a fling with a handsome barman. By week's end, Cath, Wyatt, and Amity have forged firm friendships, solved a tricky crime, and smoothed out some serious wrinkles in their own lives. VERDICT This charming and heartfelt cozy from Dukess (The Last Book Party) is just the thing for a rainy afternoon and a cup of tea. The dialogue is snappy, the characters are endearing, and the plot is satisfyingly quirky." This program will be provided by Davidson library staff. Information about this Group The Detection Book Club meets monthly to discuss Mystery and Suspense titles. For a list of all 2026 selections, please click here: Detection Book Club How to Join an Online ProgramThis event takes place on Zoom. Please register by 8:00 am the day of the event. You’ll receive an email with a secure Zoom link with a reminder about the meeting.Looking for more technology resources? Check out this list!

events.title: Mayor's Summer Reading Club

Summer 2026's book is "Dinosaur!" by Olivia Aston Bosworth, about a little boy who loves dinosaurs and sees them in everything! events.title: Flights of Fantasy: Immortal Longings
events.description: Enjoy reading fantasy? Each month, we'll discuss an exciting novel from the fantasy and sci-fi genres. —- Join Krista M. at Coffee Hub in Beavercreek to discuss this month's book Immortal Longings, a novel by Chloe Gong. Preview:"Every year, thousands in the kingdom of Talin will flock to its capital twin cities, San-Er, where the palace hosts a set of games. For those confident enough in their ability to jump between bodies, competitors across San-Er fight to the death to win unimaginable riches. Princess Calla Tuoleimi lurks in hiding. Five years ago, a massacre killed her parents and left the palace of Er empty ... and she was the one who did it. Before King Kasa's forces in San can catch her, she plans to finish the job and bring down the monarchy. Her reclusive uncle always greets the victor of the games, so if she wins, she gets her opportunity at last to kill him. Enter Anton Makusa, an exiled aristocrat. His childhood love has lain in a coma since they were both ousted from the palace, and he's deep in debt trying to keep her alive. Thankfully, he's one of the best jumpers in the kingdom, flitting from body to body at will. His last chance at saving her is entering the games and winning. Calla finds both an unexpected alliance with Anton and help from King Kasa's adopted son, August, who wants to mend Talin's ills. But the three of them have very different goals, even as Calla and Anton's partnership spirals into something all-consuming. Before the games close, Calla must decide what she's playing for--her lover or her kingdom"--

Hay dos opciones puedes venir a la biblioteca de University City o participar de forma virtual a traves del enlance de zoom que recibas al momento de registrarte. Información a cerca de este club El club de eactura en español se reúne cada mes para hablar sobre libros de diferentes géneros literarios, aunque también de vez en vez leemos libros traducidos al español de otros idiomas. ¿Cómo participar de forma virtual? Este programa se llevará a cabo en formato virtual a través de Zoom. Regístrate con tu correo electrónico para recibir el enlace de acceso al programa en Zoom al menos 24 horas antes del evento. No sabes usar Zoom? dale un vistazo a estos videos que pueden ayudarte: Zoom How-To Videos o mira esta lista this list para mas recursos.

events.title: History Book Discussion:  Evicted by Matthew Desmond
events.description: NEW YORK TIMES BESTSELLER • WINNER OF THE PULITZER PRIZE • ONE OF TIME’S TEN BEST NONFICTION BOOKS OF THE DECADE • ONE OF THE NEW YORK TIMES’S 100 BEST BOOKS OF THE 21ST CENTURY • A KIRKUS REVIEWS BEST NONFICTION BOOK OF THE CENTURY • AN OPRAH DAILY BEST NONFICTION BOOK OF THE PAST TWO DECADES In Evicted, Princeton sociologist and MacArthur “Genius” Matthew Desmond follows eight families in Milwaukee as they each struggle to keep a roof over their heads. Hailed as “wrenching and revelatory” (The Nation), “vivid and unsettling” (New York Review of Books), Evicted transforms our understanding of poverty and economic exploitation while providing fresh ideas for solving one of twenty-first-century America’s most devastating problems. Its unforgettable scenes of hope and loss remind us of the centrality of home, without which nothing else is possible.

events.title: Virtual Author Talk: Zaila Avant-garde
events.description: Join us for an uplifting virtual conversation with bestselling author and Scripps National Spelling Bee champion Zaila Avant-garde as she introduces her new picture book, Weird and Wonderful You, which encourages young readers to embrace what makes them unique. The book celebrates individuality and self-acceptance through vibrant illustrations, inspiring quotes, and affirmations that help children see their quirks as strengths. This joyful message reminds readers that being different is something to be proud of. It is an empowering read for anyone who has ever felt out of place and a perfect gift for young readers learning to appreciate the wonderfully weird parts of themselves. ---The Santa Clara County Library District is proud to present a free virtual author‑talk series featuring bestselling, award‑winning, and acclaimed writers from around the world, offering live Q&A sessions two to three times a month. Event links are sent about 24 hours in advance (or upon late registration), recordings are available for a limited time, and a full list of upcoming programs can be found here. ---For accessibility accommodations (ex. events.description: Hello Book Club Families, Book of the Week: Magic Tree House: Stage Fright on A Summer NightJack and Annie travel in their magic tree house to Elizabethan London, where they become actors in a production of A Midsummer Night's Dream and try to rescue a tame bear. About our Inclusive Book Club: Each week, an AAC user hosts the session and a librarian reads the book aloud. After the reading, we enjoy an activity or game connected to the story. We celebrate all forms of communication, including AAC, speech, gestures, and expressions. Our goal is to create a supportive and joyful space where everyone can participate in their own way. When available, we send a visual schedule 24 hours before the session to help participants follow along. Special thanks to Mary Pope Osborne and Penguin Random House for permission to share Magic Tree House #25: Stage Fright On A Summer Night as part of this program.

events.title: Swasey Book Club
events.description: Join us for the Swasey Book Club! June's selection is Framed by John Grisham and Jim McCloskey. About the book:"In his first work of nonfiction since The Innocent Man, #1 bestselling author John Grisham and Centurion Ministries Founder Jim McCloskey share ten harrowing true stories of wrongful convictions. Impeccably researched and grippingly told, Framed offers an inside look at the victims of the United States criminal justice system. A fundamental principle of our legal system is a presumption of innocence, but once someone has been found guilty there is very little room to prove doubt. Framed shares ten true stories of men who were innocent but found guilty and forced to sacrifice friends, families, wives, and decades of their lives to prison while the guilty parties remained free. In each of the stories, John Grisham and Jim McCloskey recount the dramatic hard-fought battles for exoneration." Stop into the Swasey branch to secure your book club copy and then join us for the discussion on June 16. We hope you'll join us! The Swasey Book Club selection checks out on a first-come, first-served basis. Supply is limited. Ask library staff for assistance. Call us at (253)280-2970 if you have any questions.

events.title: Cookbook Book Club
events.description: Do you love books and food? Are you looking for a fun, low-cost way to increase your cooking skills, try new recipes and make some new foodie friends? Then perhaps our new "Cookbook Book Club" is for you!Our book club hostess is Chef JoEllen Hothem. She is a self-educated home chef who began exploring food ways and cooking as a child through the "Mud Pies and Other Recipes" by Marjorie Winslow. She loves to learn about world cuisines through reading and travel. A firm believer in the tremendous health benefits of intentional, healthful eating, in community, she combines her love of food with her public speaking and instructional training to help people learn the principles of taste, flavor and foundational cooking skills, so they can confidently prepare tasty and healthful meals for themselves, family and friends.

As we dive into the book's rich history, we'll also be enjoying the very thing the restaurant does best—perfectly smoked, seasoned meats. Come hungry for both knowledge and great food!

events.title: Chapter by Chapter: Non-Fiction Book Club
events.description: Join us for a monthly gathering for seniors who want to explore the lives and stories of remarkable individuals. Each month, we dive into a new nonfiction book, uncovering the experiences, challenges, and triumphs of people who have shaped history. This club provides a welcoming space to share insights, engage in thoughtful discussions, and connect with others over the power of storytelling. Whether you're a lifelong reader or just beginning to explore biographies, this book club offers a chance to learn, reflect, and celebrate the stories that inspire us all. This month's selection: Mother, Nature by Jedidiah JenkinsIn this poignant memoir from the New York Times bestselling author of To Shake the Sleeping Self, a forty-year-old gay man and his eccentric conservative mother travel the country together and find surprising answers to our generational and cultural rifts. When his mother, Barbara, turned seventy, Jedidiah Jenkins was reminded of a palpable, sobering truth: Our parents won't live forever. For years, he and Barbara had talked about taking a trip together, just the two of them. They landed on an idea: retrace the thousands of miles Barbara trekked with Jedidiah's father, travel writer Peter Jenkins, as part of the "Walk Across America" book trilogy that became a sensation in the 1970s. They began in New Orleans and set off for the Oregon coast, listening to podcasts about outlaws and cult leaders-the only media they could agree on-while reliving the journey that changed Barbara's life. Jenkins discovers who she was as a thirty-year-old writer walking across America; who she became later, as a wife scorned by infidelity; and now, who she is as a parent who loves her son while holding on to a version of faith that sees his sexuality as a sin. Along the way, he peels back the layers of questions millions are asking today: How do we stay in relationship when it hurts? When do boundaries turn into separation? When do we stand up for ourselves, and when do we let it go? Tender, smart, and profound, Mother, Nature is a story of a remarkable mother-son bond and a moving meditation on the complexities of love Books available at the Kettering-Moraine branch. To get a sneak peek at more titles related to non-fiction and past book club selections, you can click here.

events.title: Adult Book Discussion: Harlem Rhapsody
events.description: Book Jacket: Harlem Rhapsody In June, the Adult Book Discussion Group will disuss the novel Harlem Rhapsody by Victoria Christopher Murray. (2025) Jessie Redmon Fauset becomes the first female editor of The Crisis in 1919, despite rumors of her ongoing affair with W.E.B. DuBois. Determined to prove herself, Jessie makes The Crisis one of the pre-eminent black magazines of the Harlem Renaissance, and her success means all prominent black American authors are eager to publish their work, there. But Jessie’s professional success comes with a high personal cost. The Adult Book Discussion group meets on the Third Wednesday morning of each month at 11 am, and on the third Thursday evening at 7pm in Room 201B Adults of all ages are welcome. All meetings are sponsored by The Friends of the Cupertino Library.For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@sccl.org.

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: The Very Secret Society of Irregular Witches
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Join our book club for spirited discussions on a wide range of fiction and non-fiction. Each month we'll tackle a new title. Whether you loved it, loathed it, or left it unfinished, we want to hear your take. Come share your impressions and connect with fellow readers! This week we will be reading The Very Secret Society of Irregular Witches by Sangu Mandanna. A warm and uplifting novel about an isolated witch whose opportunity to embrace a quirky new family--and a new love--changes the course of her life. As one of the few witches in Britain, Mika Moon knows she has to hide her magic, keep her head down, and stay away from other witches so their powers don't mingle and draw attention. And as an orphan who lost her parents at a young age and was raised by strangers, she's used to being alone and she follows the rules... with one exception: an online account, where she posts videos "pretending" to be a witch. She thinks no one will take it seriously. But someone does. Magic isn't the only danger in the world, and when a threat comes knocking at their door, Mika will need to decide whether to risk everything to protect a found family she didn't know she was looking for.

events.title: Books & Bars: "Parable of the Sower" by Octavia E. Butler
events.description: Books & Bars: Parable of the Sower by Octavia E. ButlerWednesday, June 176:30 - 8:00 p.m.Utepils Brewing225 Thomas Avenue North, MinneapolisFifteen-year-old Lauren Olamina lives inside a gated community with her preacher father, family, and neighbors, sheltered from the surrounding social chaos and anarchy caused by climate change and economic crisis. In a society where any vulnerability is a risk, she suffers from hyperempathy--a debilitating sensitivity to others' emotions.Precocious and clear-eyed, Lauren must make her voice heard in order to protect her loved ones from the imminent disasters her small community stubbornly ignores. But what begins as a fight for survival soon leads to something much more: the birth of a new faith and a startling vision of human destiny.

events.description: Join the Main Book Club in the Olympic Room to discuss the novel, Last Murder at the End of the World by Stuart Turton.About the book: Solve the murder to save what's left of the world.Outside the island there is nothing: the world was destroyed by a fog that swept the planet, killing anyone it touched.On the island: it is idyllic. One hundred and twenty-two villagers and three scientists, living in peaceful harmony. The villagers are content to fish, farm and feast, to obey their nightly curfew, to do what they're told by the scientists.From the bestselling author of The 7 1/2 Deaths of Evelyn Hardcastle and The Devil and the Dark Water comes an inventive, high-concept murder mystery: an ingenious puzzle, an extraordinary backdrop, and an audacious solution. events.title: High Intermediate - Advanced ESL Book Club - A Farewell to Arms
events.description: ESL Book Club will read A Farewell to Arms by Ernest Hemingway. This book club is best for high intermediate - advanced English learners. Students will read at home each week. Then you will meet as a group once a week to talk about the story, learn new vocabulary, and practice conversation. Book club will be led by Fernando Lopez. Meeting dates: May 28; June 4, 11, 18, 25; July 2, 9, 16.Meeting time: Thursdays, 10:30am - 12:00nRegistration is open now! Book club is limited to 8 participants. Books will be provided for registered participants to keep. Please consider your schedule and do not register if you will miss more than one meeting.A Farewell to Arms:Hemingway’s masterpiece is not only one of the best novels we have about World War I but is also a tender, haunting love story. Ernest Hemingway drew from his own war experiences when he crafted this remarkable story of an American ambulance driver serving on the Italian front and his love for a beautiful English nurse. The novel, Hemingway’s first best seller, is marked by vivid depictions of the horrors of the battlefield—but also by the heartrending vicissitudes of a passionate affair of the heart between his protagonists, Frederic and Catherine, leading up to a tragic ending that is all the more powerful for its famously understated expression.

events.title: Virtual Author Talk: Ted Page
events.description: Join us for a virtual conversation with storyteller and blogger Ted Page as he discusses Good Grandpa: Stories from the Heart of Grandfatherhood, a collection that brings together the wisdom and experiences of grandfathers from many backgrounds with the shared goal of supporting the next great generation. Inspired by his search for positive stories about grandfatherhood, Page launched GoodGrandpa.com and interviewed a wide range of grandfathers, including athletes, faith leaders, veterans, public figures, and everyday families, gathering meaningful insights along the way. Through these conversations, Page explores family histories and asks each grandfather to share the single most important piece of wisdom they hope to pass on. The result is a warm and inspiring portrait of modern grandfatherhood, offering hope, guidance, and heartfelt stories that will resonate with readers and become a favorite for grandparents everywhere. ---The Santa Clara County Library District is proud to present a free virtual author‑talk series featuring bestselling, award‑winning, and acclaimed writers from around the world, offering live Q&A sessions two to three times a month. Event links are sent about 24 hours in advance (or upon late registration), recordings are available for a limited time, and a full list of upcoming programs can be found here. ---For accessibility accommodations (ex. events.title: Genre Jumpers Book Club
events.description: Read a new genre every month! This month, we delve into Literary Fiction and Epistolary Novel with Ocean Vuong’s stunning debut novel, On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous. Written as a letter from a son to a mother who cannot read, this story unearths a family's history rooted in Vietnam and replanted in Hartford, Connecticut. Through the eyes of the narrator, Little Dog, the novel explores a traumatic past, a transformative first love, and the lyrical ways we attempt to heal across generational divides. Copies will be available at the Information Desk, and the e-book and e-audiobook versions are available on CloudLibrary. Preregister.On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous

events.title: Civic Center Afternoon Book Club: 'On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous' by Ocean Vuong
events.description: As part of our celebration and commemoration of Pride in June, the Civic Center Library's Afternoon Book Group will meet on Thursday, June 18th from 1:00-2:00pm to discuss On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous by Ocean Vuong, an acclaimed Vietnamese American poet and novelist who frequently explores themes of queer love, masculinity, and his identity as a gay man in his writing. This afternoon book group generally meets on the 3rd Thursday of every month from 1:00-2:00pm. All are welcome to join! Request a copy of the book online or ask at another County Library location for assistance in placing a hold. On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous (book) or On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous (eBook) or On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous (eAudiobook) or On Earth We're Briefly Gorgeous (large print) by Ocean Vuong Poet Ocean Vuong’s debut novel is a shattering portrait of a family, a first love, and the redemptive power of storytelling. On Earth We’re Briefly Gorgeous is a letter from a son to a mother who cannot read. Written when the speaker, Little Dog, is in his late twenties, the letter unearths a family’s history that began before he was born — a history whose epicenter is rooted in Vietnam — and serves as a doorway into parts of his life his mother has never known, all of it leading to an unforgettable revelation. At once a witness to the fraught yet undeniable love between a single mother and her son, it is also a brutally honest exploration of race, class, and masculinity. Asking questions central to our American moment, immersed as we are in addiction, violence, and trauma, but undergirded by compassion and tenderness, On Earth We’re Briefly Gorgeous is as much about the power of telling one’s own story as it is about the obliterating silence of not being heard. - adapted from the publisher ADA Accommodation Request | Solicitud de Adaptación ADA

events.title: Book Clubs of EPL: How to Pronounce Knife
events.description: Love reading? Love to talk about books you've read? Come join us for lively discussions on all types of fiction and non-fiction. This month we are reading How to Pronounce Knife by Souvankham Thammavongsa.Named one of Time's Must-Read Books of 2020 , and featuring stories that have appeared in Harper's, Granta, The Atlantic, and The Paris Review , this revelatory book of fiction from O. Henry Award winner Souvankham Thammavongsa establishes her as an essential new voice in Canadian and world literature. Told with compassion and wry humour, these stories honour characters struggling to find their bearings far from home, even as they do the necessary "grunt work of the world."

events.title: Adult Book Discussion: Harlem Rhapsody
events.description: Book Jacket: Harlem Rhapsody In June, the Adult Book Discussion Group will disuss the novel Harlem Rhapsody by Victoria Christopher Murray. (2025) Jessie Redmon Fauset becomes the first female editor of The Crisis in 1919, despite rumors of her ongoing affair with W.E.B. DuBois. Determined to prove herself, Jessie makes The Crisis one of the pre-eminent black magazines of the Harlem Renaissance, and her success means all prominent black American authors are eager to publish their work, there. But Jessie’s professional success comes with a high personal cost. The Adult Book Discussion group meets on the Third Wednesday morning of each month at 11 am, and on the third Thursday evening at 7pm in Room 201B Adults of all ages are welcome. All meetings are sponsored by The Friends of the Cupertino Library.For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@sccl.org.
